§ 01What This Is

Dear {{recipientName}},

Thank you for your continued interest in exploring an employee ownership transition for {{companyName}}. As the next step in our process, we are providing you with the enclosed Pre-Flight Checklist.

The Pre-Flight Checklist is a mutual readiness assessment — a structured review of the key items that must be in place before we can responsibly proceed with a formal engagement. It is designed to protect both parties by ensuring there are no material surprises after significant time and resources have been committed.

§ 02What It Covers

The checklist is organized into six parts covering 34 items across the following areas:

01Financial Readiness — QofE-normalized EBITDA, tax returns, outstanding liabilities, and capital structure.
02Operational Readiness — Management depth, key-person dependencies, and organizational structure.
03Legal & Corporate — Entity type, governance documents, pending litigation, and intellectual property.
04Employee & Benefits — Current headcount, benefit plans, and any existing equity arrangements.
05Transaction-Specific — Seller timeline expectations, desired post-close role, and deal structure preferences.
06Authorization — Confirmation to proceed, retainer acknowledgment, and signature.
§ 03The Deposit

Part 6 of the checklist includes a $15,000 non-refundable deposit. This deposit serves two purposes:

A.Commitment Signal. It confirms that you are serious about proceeding and authorizes Forhemit to begin assembling the professional team (trustee, ERISA counsel, appraiser, lender).
B.Credit Toward Retainer. The full $15,000 is credited toward your Engagement Retainer upon execution of the formal Engagement Letter. It is not an additional fee.
Important: The deposit is non-refundable. This is intentional — it compensates Forhemit for the team assembly and deal structuring work that begins immediately upon receipt. We do not take this lightly, and neither should you.
§ 04What Happens Next

Once we receive the signed checklist and deposit:

1.Forhemit will review your responses and may request clarification on specific items within 3 business days.
2.If all items are satisfactory, we will issue the formal Engagement Letter within 5 business days.
3.If any items require further discussion, we will schedule a call to address them before proceeding.
4.Upon execution of the Engagement Letter, the 120-day transaction timeline begins.

Please review the enclosed checklist carefully. If you have questions about any item, contact us before signing — we are happy to walk through it together.

§ 05Contact

If you have questions about the Pre-Flight Checklist or the transaction process, please contact:

deals@forhemit.com
{{ref}}

Please reference the above number in all correspondence related to this assessment.

—Forhemit Transition Stewardship
Forhemit Stewardship Management Co.
California Public Benefit Corporation
Authorized Representative
